title: 2 Boston bars earn spots on 50 Best’s top 100 U.S. bars list
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: The Wig Shop, at 27 Temple Place, ranks no. 86 on the 50 Best's list of top 100 U.S. bars.
Hecate, located at 48 Gloucester St., holds the 88th spot on the prestigious list.
Both Boston bars, known for craft cocktails, are new entries on the 50 Best list for 2026.

title: Mass. man sentenced for online threats to public figures, children, elementary school
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Funwayo Mbilini Nyawo, 37, from Wilbraham, was sentenced to 32 months for making violent threats online.
Nyawo pleaded guilty to 13 counts of interstate threatening communications and stalking using interstate facilities.
Posts targeted officials, police, children, and schools, often referencing violence like shootings and bombings.

title: The Liberty Hotel unveils a $75K World Cup ultra-luxe package
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Liberty Hotel in Boston offers a $75,000 World Cup package including a stay in the Ebersol Presidential Suite.
The package features premium World Cup match seats, helicopter transport, autographed jerseys, and a personal concierge.
Available during World Cup match dates, the package requires a three-night stay at $25,000 per night.

title: Umpire admits he forgot the count during bizarre Red Sox sequence
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Umpire Mark Wegner admitted forgetting the count, allowing Houston's Cam Smith to avoid a strikeout in a Red Sox game.
Smith's at-bat continued after the missed call, contributing to advances and scoring errors by Red Sox players.
The mistake occurred during the Red Sox's 9-2 loss to the Astros, with pitcher Brayan Bello conceding five earned runs.

title: Hudson man accused of beating grandmother to death with skateboard, also injuring father and sister
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Devin Dube, 29, of Hudson, allegedly killed his grandmother with a skateboard and injured his father and sister.
The incident occurred in the family home on Munson Street; Dube was arrested nearby in a van shortly after fleeing.
Dube will be arraigned on charges including murder and multiple counts of assault; victims received treatment and were released.

title: Former Rev Esmir Bajraktarević scored winning penalty in World Cup playoff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Esmir Bajraktarević, former New England Revolution player, scored the decisive penalty kick for Bosnia, eliminating Italy from World Cup.
Bosnia and Herzegovina are among six teams, including Türkiye and Iraq, that secured World Cup spots through playoff victories.
Iraq's win against Bolivia qualifies them for their first World Cup since 1986, with a match against Norway at 'Boston Stadium'.

title: Medford schools to close on Good Friday due to staffing shortages
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Medford schools will close on April 3 due to staffing shortages linked to religious observances and personal absences.
Superintendent Suzanne Galusi announced the closure, citing insufficient staffing for safe school operations and learning.
School officials will adjust the end-of-year schedule to accommodate the missed day as per district requirements.

title: 5 restaurants we can’t wait to try in April
author: Jillian Dara
bullets: Avra Estiatorio, a modern Greek restaurant, opens mid-April in Boston's Back Bay with Mediterranean seafood and coastal decor.
Dalia, a Spanish-inspired restaurant in South Boston, offers wood-fired Basque cooking and a dedicated gin and tonic section.
La Tavernetta introduces Southern Italian cuisine with waterfront dining in East Boston, opening early to mid-April.

title: Snowboarder sustains serious injuries in Cannon Mountain crash
author: John Waller
bullets: A 36-year-old Ashland man was seriously injured snowboarding out of bounds at Cannon Mountain, New Hampshire.
The snowboarder struck a tree in a steep, rocky section and required ski patrol and Fish and Game assistance.
Rescue involved using ropes due to terrain; he was hospitalized after being transported in a private vehicle.

title: Boston police investigate stabbing near Northeastern University
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston police are investigating a stabbing near Northeastern University on St. Botolph Street reported Wednesday morning.
Police confirmed the wounded individual, not affiliated with Northeastern, was hospitalized; their condition is unclear.
Authorities are searching for the suspect, believed to have fled the scene; no arrests have been made.
